source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Consider ACK Sandboxed-Container for running untrusted workloads

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Consider ACK Sandboxed-Container for running untrusted workloads
input_row.description: Use	ACK	Sandboxed-Container	to	restrict	untrusted	workloads	as	an	additional	layer	of protection	when	running	in	a	multi-tenant	environment.
input_row.rationale: Sandboxed-Container	provides	an	alternative	to	the	Docker	runtime.	It	allows	you	to	run applications	in	a	sandboxed	and	lightweight	virtual	machine	that	has	a	dedicated	kernel. This	enhances	resource	isolation	and	improves	security.	Compared	with	Docker, SandboxedContainer	has	the	following	benefits: • Strong	isolation	based	on	sandboxed	and	lightweight	virtual	machines. • Network	Attached	Storage	(NAS)	file	systems	and	Alibaba	Cloud	disks	can	be mounted	to	Sandboxed-Container.	This	provides	the	same	storage	performance	as storage	volumes	that	are	mounted	to	the	host. • Compatibility	with	runC	in	terms	of	application	management. • High	performance	that	corresponds	to	90%	performance	of	applications	based	on runC. • The	same	user	experience	as	runC	in	terms	of	logging,	monitoring,	and	storage. • Support	for	RuntimeClass. • Easy	to	use	with	limited	expertise	that	is	required	to	use	virtual	machines. • Higher	stability	than	that	provided	by	Kata	Containers.
input_row.audit: Remediation: For	create	cluster: 1. Log	on	to	the	ACK	console	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/. 2. In	the	left-side	navigation	pane,	click	Clusters. 3. On	the	Clusters	page,	click	Create	Kubernetes	Cluster	in	the	upper-right	corner	of the	page. 4. On	the	Select	Cluster	Template	page,	find	Standard	Managed	Cluster	and	click	Create in	the	Standard	Managed	Cluster	section. 275	|	P a g e 5. On	the	ACK	managed	edition	tab,	configure	the	cluster	and	set	the	parameters	as required	in	the	following	list: • Zone:	Select	a	zone	where	Elastics	Compute	Service	(ECS)	Bare	Metal	instances	are deployed.	Sandboxed	containers	support	only	ECS	Bare	Metal	instances. • Kubernetes	Version:	Select	1.18.8-aliyun.1	or	later. • Container	Runtime:	Select	Sandboxed-Container. • Worker	Instance:	Add	worker	nodes	by	creating	new	ECS	instances. • Billing	Method:	Select	the	subscription	billing	method. • Instance	Type:	Select	ECS	Bare	Metal	instances. • Mount	Data	Disk:	Mount	a	data	disk	of	at	least	200	GiB.	We	recommend	that	you mount	a	data	disk	of	at	least	1	TB. • Operating	system:	By	default,	the	AliyunLinux	operating	system	is	used.	You	cannot change	the	operating	system. Default	Value: By	default,	Sandboxed	container	is	disabled References: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/142151.htm 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/140541.htm 3. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/160288.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 18.9	Separate	Production	and	Non-Production	Systems Maintain	separate	environments	for	production	and	nonproduction	systems.	Developers should	not	have	unmonitored	access	to	production	environments. 276	|	P a g e
input_row.references: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/142151.htm 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/140541.htm 3. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/160288.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 18.9	Separate	Production	and	Non-Production	Systems Maintain	separate	environments	for	production	and	nonproduction	systems.	Developers should	not	have	unmonitored	access	to	production	environments. 276	|	P a g e
input_row.justification: While certain aspects of this control—such as verifying the container runtime for a cluster—can be automated using Alibaba Cloud CLI or API, the core requirement is to "consider" and apply ACK Sandboxed-Container specifically for "untrusted workloads." This involves subjective judgment about workload trust levels, business context, and security policy, which cannot be determined programmatically. There is no API or CLI method to automatically identify which workloads are "untrusted" or to confirm that Sandboxed-Container is being used for them according to organizational policy.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. **Access the ACK Console:** - Log in to the Alibaba Cloud ACK console at https://cs.console.aliyun.com/. - In the left navigation pane, click "Clusters." 2. **Review Cluster Configuration:** - Select the relevant Kubernetes cluster. - Check the "Container Runtime" setting to verify if "Sandboxed-Container" is enabled. - Confirm the cluster is running on ECS Bare Metal instances and is using Kubernetes version 1.18.8-aliyun.1 or later. 3. **Assess Workload Assignment:** - Review organizational security policies and documentation to determine which workloads are classified as "untrusted." - Cross-reference these workloads with the cluster(s) or node pools configured to use Sandboxed-Container. 4. **Confirm Compliance:** - Ensure that all untrusted workloads are deployed exclusively to clusters or node pools with Sandboxed-Container enabled. - Document evidence (screenshots, policy references, cluster settings) for audit purposes.
input_row.default_value: By default, Sandboxed container is disabled

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The decision is made to take a manual approach due to the need for assessing the application of ACK Sandboxed-Container based on subjective criteria (e.g., what constitutes "untrusted workloads") that cannot be determined programmatically. While some configurations can be validated through APIs, the core requirement necessitates human judgment regarding workload classification and compliance with organizational policies.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access the ACK Console:**
   - Log in to the Alibaba Cloud ACK console at [https://cs.console.aliyun.com/](https://cs.console.aliyun.com/).
   - In the left navigation pane, click **Clusters**.

2. **Review Cluster Configuration:**
   - Select the relevant Kubernetes cluster.
   - Check the **Container Runtime** setting to verify if **Sandboxed-Container** is enabled.
   - Confirm the following cluster configurations:
     - Ensure the cluster is running on **ECS Bare Metal** instances.
     - Verify that the Kubernetes version is set to **1.18.8-aliyun.1** or later.

3. **Assess Workload Assignment:**
   - Review organizational security policies and documentation to determine which workloads are classified as "untrusted."
   - Cross-reference these workloads with the cluster(s) or node pools configured to use Sandboxed-Container.

4. **Confirm Compliance:**
   - Ensure that all identified untrusted workloads are deployed exclusively to clusters or node pools with Sandboxed-Container enabled.
   - Document evidence (screenshots, policy references, cluster settings) for audit purposes.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** PARTIALLY_AGREE

- The manual approach is justified due to the subjective nature of determining what constitutes "untrusted workloads" based on organizational policies. However, some aspects of this control, such as verifying if the Sandboxed-Container is enabled and checking the Kubernetes version, can be automated using Alibaba Cloud APIs or the Aliyun CLI.
- Alibaba Cloud provides APIs and CLI commands that can be used to retrieve cluster configurations, including the container runtime and Kubernetes version. Therefore, a hybrid approach (combining manual and automated steps) might be more efficient.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 7

- The manual steps are generally accurate and provide a clear process for checking compliance. However, they do not leverage available automation capabilities for certain checks.
- The use of the Aliyun CLI or Alibaba Cloud SDK could automate the verification of cluster configurations, such as checking if Sandboxed-Container is enabled and ensuring the Kubernetes version is compliant.
- The program name convention is not applicable here since the assessment is manual, but if automation were implemented, a descriptive name like `alicloud_ack_sandboxed_container_check` would be appropriate.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Automation Opportunities:** 
  - Use the Aliyun CLI to check if Sandboxed-Container is enabled:
    ```bash
    aliyun cs DescribeClusterDetail --ClusterId <cluster-id>
    ```
    This command can retrieve details about the cluster, including the container runtime.
  - Verify the Kubernetes version using:
    ```bash
    aliyun cs DescribeClusterDetail --ClusterId <cluster-id> | grep "kubernetes_version"
    ```
- **Program Name Suggestion:** If automation is implemented, use a descriptive name like `alicloud_ack_sandboxed_container_check`.
- **Documentation:** Include references to Alibaba Cloud documentation for using the CLI and APIs to enhance the audit process.

**CONFIDENCE:** MEDIUM

- While the manual steps are well-defined, the lack of automation for parts of the process that could benefit from it reduces confidence in the efficiency of the assessment.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- **Modify:** Implement a hybrid approach that combines manual and automated steps. Use Alibaba Cloud APIs or the Aliyun CLI to automate the verification of technical configurations, such as checking if Sandboxed-Container is enabled and verifying the Kubernetes version. Retain manual steps for assessing organizational policies and workload classification.
